steps.title: Identify the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage follows a clear pattern: it defines a research approach (community science), explains why it's valuable (by listing specific benefits), and then shows how it worked in practice (through Merrill's study).

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly identifies the passage's structure—approach defined, benefits listed, study mentioned.
The others either misrepresent what the passage does (B claims argumentation and “public opinion,” C invents development and future projects, D claims results are presented) or skip important elements.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
